title: calamagrostis affinis
intro: Calamagrostis affinis, also known as Himalayan feather grass or lesser fescue, is a species of grass native to the Himalayas and other mountainous regions in Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Calamagrostis affinis are numerous, including the potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
